_comment: STAGED, DIAGNOSE-ONLY backlog of verified July-2026 volatile-domain deltas (P47). NOTHING here is applied by committing this file; each entry names the exact, unexecuted command a human runs to apply it. This file is a registry SIBLING, not the registry, so committing it does not change the freshness canonical_digest and does not trip invariant 26. Priority order: highest first. 'confidence' is verified_live (re-checked against the source on 2026-07-14), verified (checked in the July-2026 research pass), or needs_verification (carry the flag until re-checked at apply).

corrections.problem: requirements-mcp.txt pins `mcp` with no upper bound while the breaking major mcp 2.x line (2.0.0a1..2.0.0b2) is published on PyPI; a future `mcp==2.0.0` stable would silently install and break the server.
corrections.apply_command:
python3 - <<'PY'
import pathlib
p=pathlib.Path('requirements-mcp.txt'); t=p.read_text().splitlines()
t=[('mcp>=1.28,<2' if l.strip()=='mcp' else l) for l in t]
p.write_text('\n'.join(t)+'\n')
PY

corrections.problem: The registry source `dep-mcp` has validated_version=null (only latest_seen=1.28.1), so the drift checker has no known-good baseline to compare against.
corrections.apply_command: python3 tools/dependency_currency.py check --apply --only dep-mcp   # after requirements-mcp.txt is pinned; then re-check that validated_version is populated
corrections.apply_verification: [NEEDS VERIFICATION at apply] dependency_currency --apply stamps last_checked/latest_seen but may not write validated_version; if it does not, upsert the dep-mcp record via `source_currency.py seed-sources` with validated_version set. Never hand-edit the registry.

corrections.problem: The New York synthetic-performer disclosure law (S8420-A) took effect 2026-06-09, but its registry source ny-synthetic-performer-law has last_checked=null (never re-verified since it took effect). Surfaced by drift invariant 43 and moving-dates.json.
corrections.target: Re-verify the source, then mark it checked-and-changed so the affected atoms are flagged.

corrections.problem: The TikTok importer + docs carry a [NEEDS VERIFICATION] on rate-limit / total-video ceiling; the documented limit is 600 requests/minute per endpoint with no published total-video cap, so the 250-page importer backstop is a defensive default, not a documented limit.
corrections.target: Resolve the [NEEDS VERIFICATION] note in tools/importers/tiktok_import.py and docs/CONTENT-IMPORT.md to state 600 req/min per endpoint, no documented total-video cap; the page cap stays a defensive backstop surfaced as truncation.
corrections.apply_command: # doc/comment edit only: update the [NEEDS VERIFICATION] rate-limit note in tools/importers/tiktok_import.py and docs/CONTENT-IMPORT.md

corrections.problem: The EU AI Act Article 50 transparency / AIGC-labelling obligations apply 2026-08-02 and are not yet a tracked source. This is the only genuinely new source from the July-2026 verification.
corrections.target: Seed the proposed source in canonical-sources/eu-ai-act-seed.json, then re-stamp the freshness baseline (adding a source id moves the digest).
corrections.apply_command: see canonical-sources/eu-ai-act-seed.json apply_command (seed the source, then python3 tools/build_freshness_bundle.py --apply)

corrections.problem: California SB 942 (amended by AB 853) operative date 2026-08-02 is confirmed; a phase-2 obligation set takes effect 2027-01-01 and was not separately tracked.
corrections.target: Already captured as a phase_2 date in moving-dates.json (ca-ai-transparency-sb942); no registry change needed until 2026-08-02 approaches.
corrections.apply_command: # no action until the operative date nears; moving-dates.json already tracks both dates

corrections.problem: The current MCP specification revision is 2025-11-25; any reference to an older spec revision is stale.
corrections.target: Update any MCP-spec revision reference to 2025-11-25 where one is cited.
corrections.apply_command: # doc edit only where an MCP spec revision is referenced

corrections.problem: Several records verified as unchanged in July 2026 carry stale last_checked dates: whisper.cpp v1.9.1, faster-whisper 1.2.1, the three smaller GGML model hashes (byte-identical), Instagram Graph API v25.0, the YouTube Data/Analytics endpoints + retention dimensions, and Pinterest API v5.
corrections.target: mark-checked (WITHOUT --changed) each verified-unchanged source so its freshness clock resets without flagging downstream atoms.
corrections.apply_command: # for each verified-unchanged source id: python3 tools/source_currency.py mark-checked <id>

retained_needs_verification.note: a v26.0 may exist; v25.0 is the pinned, verified version. Secondary; re-check before relying.
secondary source; not load-bearing. Re-check before citing.

retained_needs_verification.item: plan-gated ChatGPT / Claude / Gemini surface behaviors
retained_needs_verification.note: surface capabilities are plan-gated and change without notice; treat as provisional.

retained_needs_verification.item: the three large GGML hashes (medium, large-v3, large-v3-turbo)
retained_needs_verification.note: not re-fetched this pass; the whisper-models.json values stand until re-verified.
